evals.name: new-feature-email-validation
evals.prompt: Add email validation to the user registration function. It should reject empty emails, emails without an @ sign, and emails with spaces. The project uses pytest.
evals.expected_output: A failing test is written first for each validation rule, then minimum implementation code is added to pass each test, with refactoring assessed after each green phase
expectations: Writes a failing test before writing any production/implementation code
Runs the test after writing it and confirms it fails before implementing
The test failure is because the feature is missing, not due to a syntax error or import error
Writes minimum code to make the failing test pass — no extra validation rules beyond what the current test demands
Runs the test again after implementation to confirm it passes
Follows multiple RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycles — one per validation rule (empty, missing @, spaces) rather than implementing all at once
Assesses whether refactoring is needed after each green phase
All tests pass at the end of the workflow with clean output
anti_expectations: Does not write implementation code before a failing test exists
Does not implement all three validation rules at once before writing tests
Does not skip running the test in the RED phase to confirm failure
Does not add anticipatory features beyond what failing tests demand (e.g., DNS validation, disposable email checking)

evals.name: bug-fix-duplicate-entries
evals.prompt: There's a bug where duplicate items are being added to the shopping cart when the user clicks 'Add to Cart' quickly. Fix it using TDD. The project uses Jest for testing.
evals.expected_output: A reproduction test is written first that demonstrates the duplicate-entry bug, then a minimal fix is implemented to make the test pass
expectations: Investigates the relevant code before writing any test or fix — reads the cart logic to understand actual behavior
Writes a reproduction test that demonstrates the duplicate-entry bug before writing any fix
Runs the reproduction test and confirms it fails with the same symptom as the reported bug
Fixes the bug with minimum code to make the reproduction test pass
Runs all tests after the fix to confirm nothing else broke
Does not modify production code before the reproduction test fails
Assesses refactoring after the fix is green
anti_expectations: Does not fix the bug first and write tests afterward
Does not skip the investigation step and jump straight to coding a fix
Does not write a test that passes immediately without demonstrating the bug

evals.name: refactor-extract-helper-with-existing-tests
evals.prompt: The formatCurrency function in utils.ts is 45 lines long with duplicated logic for different locales. Refactor it into smaller functions. Tests already exist in utils.test.ts.
evals.expected_output: Existing tests are run first as a safety net, then structural changes are made one at a time with tests confirmed green after each step
expectations: Runs all existing tests first before making any structural changes to confirm they pass
Makes one structural change at a time (extract, rename, move) rather than rewriting everything at once
Runs tests after each individual refactoring step to confirm they still pass
Does not add new behavior during the refactoring — only changes structure
Does not add new tests during the refactoring phase (new tests belong in the next RED phase)
If a test breaks during refactoring, undoes the change and takes a smaller step
anti_expectations: Does not rewrite the entire function in one step without running tests between changes
Does not add new features or behavior under the guise of refactoring
Does not skip running tests between structural changes
